name: Iran
summary: Day 106 / 2026-06-13 (Saturday). **THE DEAL HAS A SIGNING DATE — SUNDAY 14 JUN, PER TRUMP — BUT THE 'FINAL TEXT' IS CONTESTED, THE SUPREME LEADER'S APPROVAL IS THIRD-HAND, AND ENRICHMENT IS UNRESOLVED.** A draft was reportedly agreed 12 Jun (two-part: an MoU on ceasefire/Hormuz/blockade, then a peace deal), and the ceasefire largely held into the weekend (no major new strikes 12-13 Jun). But the optimism sits over real fractures: Iranian media published a competing draft (US withdrawals, reconstruction funding) Trump rejected; a sitting lawmaker branded the parliament text 'irresponsible' over enrichment; Iran calls the reports 'preliminary'; and the US still demands 'zero enrichment.' Iran intends to keep a Hormuz revenue role even in the deal ('service fees' ~$1/bbl, not 'tolls'), and Trump's claim that Mojtaba Khamenei approved is attributed to an INTERMEDIARY — implausible to confirm in real time given Khamenei's courier-based comms since the 28 Feb assassination of his father. This is the 9th 'deal close' claim of the war, now with a date but the most contested text yet. Sunday's signing (or slip) is the binary; treat the deal as unsigned until a single agreed text is published.

recent_signals.headline: THE INTERIM DEAL IS SIGNED AND THE 14-POINT TEXT IS PUBLISHED. Trump (with Vance) signed electronically + formally at the G7/Versailles (17 Jun); the in-person Bürgenstock ceremony + implementation talks ran 19 Jun with Qatar/Pakistan mediators. Decisively, the FULL TEXT IS PUBLIC — Pezeshkian posted it on X, NBC and i24NEWS published copies. Terms: Hormuz reopening (free passage 60 days), US blockade lifted immediately + forces out in 30 days, end of war 'on all fronts including Lebanon', release of sanctioned funds, and a 60-day nuclear track. The war-ending interim deal is a FACT; the nuclear core is explicitly deferred. CONFIRMED (NBC/i24 text + both governments).

Signing CONFIRMED for Friday 19 Jun at Bürgenstock, Switzerland -- Swiss Foreign Ministry on record, Qatar + Pakistan attending as co-mediators (Bloomberg). Interim MoU text 'finalized' (REPORTED). BUT enrichment unresolved: AEOI head says Iran won't accept enrichment limits and uranium 'will under no circumstances be transferred' vs US 'zero enrichment' -- the nuclear core deferred to the 60-day track. Araghchi: continued Israeli occupation of Lebanon = MoU violation.

Iran confirmed the US naval blockade is lifting and tankers transiting Hormuz — the concrete deliverable Tehran can show domestically (CONFIRMED blockade-lift / REPORTED transit). Trump declared the deal 'now complete'; formal signing still 19 Jun Switzerland, MoU text still UNSEEN. Iran continues IAEA non-cooperation (10 Jun IAEA Board resolution urged cooperation; Iran denying access) — nuclear track deferred and contested.

DOMESTIC HARDLINER REVOLT ESCALATES. Hardliners staged street protests in Tehran and Mashhad against FM Araghchi and Speaker Ghalibaf over the framework; Kayhan editor Shariatmadari attacked it as surrendering Iran's best bargaining chip; Iran circulated a 10-point counter-proposal demanding recognition of its sovereign right to enrich. REPORTED (incl. Israeli/diaspora outlets). The 06-13 single-dissenter signal has become a visible bloc opposing the enrichment terms.

FRAMEWORK MoU ANNOUNCED, NOT YET SIGNED. On 14 Jun the US and Iran announced an initial framework/MoU (Trump + Pakistani PM Sharif; Iran deputy FM confirmed): ceasefire +60 days, Hormuz to reopen within ~30d 'under Iranian arrangements', US naval blockade lifted within ~30d, Lebanon hostilities to end, nuclear/enrichment DEFERRED to a later 60-day track. CRITICAL: text remains UNSEEN, formal signing now scheduled 19 Jun in Switzerland (Doha prep meetings this week). This is real de-escalation but NOT the 'published signed text' the signed-deal trigger requires. CONFIRMED (framework announced) / CONTESTED (whether signed; US officials say 'signed', NPR/Pakistan say 'to be signed Fri').

DEAL MOVED TO A NAMED SUNDAY (14 JUN) SIGNING DATE — BUT THE TEXT IS CONTESTED AND IRAN IS INTERNALLY SPLIT. A 'final draft text' was reportedly agreed 12 Jun (two-part: MoU then peace deal), and Trump says it signs Sunday. But Iranian media published a draft with DIFFERENT terms (US withdrawals, reconstruction funding) that Trump rejected, and lawmaker Meisam Zohurian called the parliament-media text 'irresponsible' over implied enrichment recognition. FM Araghchi says the MoU covers nuclear/sanctions/Hormuz; other Iranian voices say nuclear is a SEPARATE track. Iran calls the reports 'preliminary.' Ceasefire largely held (no major new strikes 12-13 Jun).
recent_signals.verification: REPORTED (deal close/draft); CLAIMED (final text, Sunday signing); CONFIRMED (ceasefire held)

recent_signals.headline: DE-ESCALATION PIVOT — DEAL 'NEVER CLOSER' BUT UNSIGNED. FM Araghchi told state TV the US-Iran agreement has 'never been closer,' an MoU 'could happen within 1 or 2 days,' describing a TWO-STAGE deal (MoU then negotiations) with a ceasefire 'on all fronts, including Lebanon.' Reiterated nuclear programme 'isn't part of discussions at this stage'; restated the war must end 'on all fronts, including Lebanon'; and — the cleanest new Iranian-side intelligence — said of Washington: 'within just a few hours you can encounter completely different, and in many cases contradictory, positions,' publicly weaponising Trump's Saturday→Sunday→Monday oscillation as a negotiating point and a justification for Iran's 30-60-day procedural floor. Rubio matched the cooling (CNN liveblog May 25): negotiations 'still a work in progress,' with 'a pretty solid thing on the table' on Hormuz. Both sides converging on 'not imminent.' (2) **Doha delegation + frozen-asset money track (CONFIRMED ACTION — Euronews May 25 primary, Daily Sabah, Detroit News/AP, Press TV, CNN)**: a high-level Iranian delegation led by Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, including FM Abbas Araghchi and Central Bank of Iran governor Abdolnaser Hemmati, arrived in Doha May 25 for talks focused on 'issues related to the Strait of Hormuz and highly enriched uranium' and the unfreezing of Iranian assets, meeting Qatar's Prime Minister. Per Fars, 'Washington has agreed to release part of Tehran's frozen overseas assets' in exchange for lifting Hormuz restrictions and the naval blockade. **The presence of the central-bank governor confirms the frozen-asset mechanics are now a live, technical negotiating workstream** — not just rhetoric — and a material operational upgrade of the Qatar track (yesterday a Qatari team was in Tehran; today Iran's economic and diplomatic principals are in Doha). (3) **Mojtaba HEU-disposal contradiction persists (REPORTED vs CLAIMED-CONTESTED)**: US sources (Axios) say the Iranians gave assurances 'verbal and in writing' that any subsequent permanent deal will include the 'disposal' of all enriched uranium (higher and lower levels), and that Mojtaba Khamenei 'endorsed the broad template' (final approval open). Against this, Baghaei said nuclear 'isn't part of discussions at this stage.' **Iranian state media has STILL neither confirmed nor walked back the Mojtaba HEU-retention directive — Day 4 of the 14-day window** (toward June 5); the negative-space signal continues. Pezeshkian separately said Iran 'is ready to assure the world it is not pursuing nuclear weapons' — a non-proliferation assurance rhetorically compatible with both keeping and disposing of the stockpile, i.e., it resolves nothing. (4) **Trump 'mandatory' Abraham Accords condition bolted onto the deal (RHETORIC / NEGOTIATING DEMAND — CONFIRMED Trump Truth Social posts via NPR May 25, WaPo, CNBC, ToI, Axios; no signed accords, no named-country acceptance)**: Trump wrote 'Negotiations with the Islamic Republic of Iran are proceeding nicely!' and declared it 'should be mandatory' that six Muslim nations — Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Pakistan, Egypt, Jordan, Turkey — 'simultaneously, sign onto the Abraham Accords,' starting with Saudi and Qatar, and invited Iran to join the 'World Coalition' if it signs. Per Axios's Ravid, Trump floated this on Saturday's leaders' call; the reaction was 'silence on the line.' This is the operative emergence of the previously-flagged Lindsey Graham 'expansionist' vector — now Trump policy — and it vastly increases the deal's degrees of freedom. (5) **Iran drone claim (CLAIMED, low significance)**: Iranian media reported the military intercepted an Israeli 'Orbiter' surveillance drone over Hormozgan province (near the Hormuz coast) May 24; the IDF said it was 'not familiar with the incident.' Intelligence value low; if true it would indicate continued Israeli ISR over southern Iran during the negotiation pause. One short signal, NO weight change. (6) **Rial firmer (REPORTED — operational parallel-market data)**: AlanChand/market May 24 put USD at ~1,707,000 IRR, down ~4.8% on the week (rial strengthened) versus the ~1,780-1,810K range carried May 24, on deal anticipation. Six-month change remains ~+50% (rial weaker yoy) but near-term direction reversed; the 2,000,000/USD black-market hyperinflation trigger remains untriggered and the linear ETA has pushed further out. Hemmati — now in Doha negotiating the frozen-asset release — is the central-bank principal whose mandate is to defend it. **Vali Nasr 'both sides slow' thesis reinforced**: Trump held 'no rush' Monday, Baghaei publicly defended the 30-60-day procedural floor and weaponised Trump's own volatility. **Sadjadpour coordinated-multi-axis posture now has an explicit economic axis** (Hemmati in Doha): Iran is signalling it can sign a partial, sequenced deal (Hormuz + assets + ceasefire) while keeping nuclear and Hormuz-management ambiguous and Lebanon a hard precondition. **MAY 25 IS RHETORIC/DIPLOMATIC POSTURE, NOT CONFIRMED KINETIC ACTION.**

recent_signals.headline: **FARS REBUTTAL DAY: IRAN'S SEMI-STATE IRGC-LINKED OUTLET STATED 'STRAIT OF HORMUZ WILL STAY UNDER IRAN'S MANAGEMENT'; VESSEL COUNTS RETURNING TO PRE-WAR LEVELS 'IN NO WAY MEANS FREE PASSAGE AS IT EXISTED BEFORE THE WAR' — DIRECTLY CONTRADICTS TRUMP'S 'STRAIT OF HORMUZ WILL BE OPENED.' BAGHAEI: NUCLEAR 'NOT A FOCUS OF PEACE TALKS AT THIS STAGE'; 30-60 DAY MOU TIMELINE 'ONLY BEGINS AFTER DOCUMENT FORMALLY AGREED.' PEZESHKIAN FORMULATION ('EFFECTIVE AND PROFESSIONAL MONITORING AND CONTROL MECHANISMS') LANDS CLOSER TO FARS THAN TO TRUMP. MAY 24 STRIKE-WINDOW EXPIRED WITHOUT KINETIC ACTION.** (1) **Fars News Agency (semi-official IRGC-linked) Sunday May 24** (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel May 23 liveblog primary, The Hill, Pravda Trump aggregator): 'Although Iran has agreed to allow the number of passing vessels to return to pre-war levels, this in no way means free passage as it existed before the war.' Iran's primary public position on the leak text. Fars also called Trump's Saturday 'largely negotiated' Truth Social post 'inconsistent with reality.' (2) **Baghaei (via Press TV / IRNA / Mehr News, CONFIRMED RHETORIC)**: 'nuclear issues are not a focus of the peace talks at this stage'; Iran 'not yet accepted any actions on its nuclear programme'; '30 to 60 days' window for finalizing MOU details ONLY BEGINS once the document is formally agreed — operational floor under any US 'within 7 days' framing. **Direct contradiction of US admin senior source 'in principle agreed to HEU disposal' framing.** (3) **Pezeshkian (Mehr News May 24)**: 'navigation conditions in the Strait of Hormuz will return to normal' with 'effective and professional monitoring and control mechanisms… within the framework of international law' — formulation lands closer to Fars 'management' framing than to Trump 'opened' framing. Civilian government coordinating with IRGC-linked outlet on sovereignty axis. (4) **Mojtaba uranium directive Day 3 of 14** toward June 5 deadline — NO Iranian state-media confirmation OR walk-back. Baghaei's implicit alignment with directive substance, no explicit attribution. (5) **Iran's 60-day MOU timeline now operationally embedded in leak text reconstruction** (REPORTED — Axios Ravid primary; FT, Al Jazeera, TOI, Xinhua secondary): (a) 60-day duration extendable by mutual consent; (b) Hormuz reopened to pre-war vessel counts with Iran clearing mines, US lifting naval blockade, 'relief for performance' principle; (c) sanctions waivers during 60-day window; (d) Iranian frozen-asset unfreezing (Iran wants immediate, US insists post-verification); (e) Nuclear track: Iran commits never to pursue weapons + negotiate enrichment suspension + negotiate HEU removal, details deferred to 60-day window; (f) Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire integrated into MOU. Baghaei specifically anchored: the 30-60 days 'only begins once document is formally agreed upon.' (6) **Munir COAS Tehran visit operationally concluded Saturday May 23** — Iranian-side framing (Baghaei: 'reduction in disputes, but issues remain to be discussed through mediators') notably more guarded than Pakistan ISPR 'encouraging progress.' (7) **Qatari negotiating team in Tehran since Friday May 22** (REPORTED — Reuters via JPost) — dual-channel architecture operationally explicit with parallel delegations. Pezeshkian's bilateral with Iraqi PM Al-Zaidi on Saturday + Pakistan COAS Munir + Qatari team = three parallel mediation tracks active in Tehran simultaneously. (8) **Tasnim 'preparing for resuming fighting' military-source piece NOT FOLLOWED UP Sunday** — modest cooling from Saturday's aggressive framing; deal track still live. (9) **IRGC global-offensive rhetoric NOT amplified Sunday** — consistent with regime ceasefire-track preservation while preserving sovereignty floor. (10) **Iran's four-axis institutional architecture publicly visible**: Fars sovereignty-maximalist + Baghaei procedural + Pezeshkian formula-finder + Mojtaba directive-via-leak. Hormuz sovereignty is the ONE axis where regime is publicly unified-defiant. (11) **AIS shipping data unchanged**: ~5-6 transits/day vs 138 historical baseline; Fars's 'agreed to allow pre-war vessel counts' is forward-looking commitment with no operational reopening through Sunday evening Tehran time. (12) **Rial AlanChand May 22 IRR/INR 18,600** (+0.54% d/d); cross-implied IRR/USD ~1,780-1,810K — consistent with last-verified 1,800-1,825K range; deal-anticipation continues to cap weakening pace; 2M trigger untriggered. **Vali Nasr 'time on Iran's side' thesis operationally validated** by Trump's adoption of literally the same 'time is on our side' language within 24 hours of his own 'decide tomorrow' framing — the cross-side timeline gap collapsed not by Iran moving fast but by Trump moving slow. **Sadjadpour 'two hubris' frame at maximum institutional fanout** with four-axis coordinated posture now publicly visible. **The single most architecturally consequential dispute in the deal text**: Fars 'management' vs Trump 'opened' is the public verbalisation of exactly the Iran-Oman selective-toll mechanism that activated the May 22 toll-mechanism trigger (Day 3 of 14, by June 5). If MOU operationally institutionalises Iran's selective-management role, Rubio's May 21 red line is breached in text; if MOU language closes that loophole, Iran's domestic propaganda position is breached. BOTH sides currently believe their version is in the leak text.

recent_signals.headline: **IRAN'S INSTITUTIONAL ARCHITECTURE FANNED OUT AT MAXIMUM AMPLITUDE WHILE PAKISTAN'S COAS MUNIR WAS IN TEHRAN: PEZESHKIAN/ARAGHCHI ENGAGED PROCEDURALLY, GHALIBAF/UN MISSION RHETORICALLY HARDENED, TASNIM REPORTED 'IRAN PREPARING FOR RESUMING FIGHTING.' BAGHAEI ANCHORED FIRST PUBLICLY-ARTICULATED IRANIAN TIMELINE: 30-60 DAYS FOR MOU FINALIZATION. MOJTABA DIRECTIVE DAY 2 OF 14: NO STATE-MEDIA CONFIRMATION OR WALK-BACK.** (1) **Munir COAS Tehran visit May 22-23** (CONFIRMED ACTION — Al Jazeera primary, ARY News, ISPR primary, Geo TV, Daily Pakistan, Times of Israel multi-source); met Pezeshkian + Ghalibaf + Araghchi + Momeni. ISPR readout: 'short but highly productive' with 'encouraging progress towards a final understanding' on ending the war (REPORTED framing). (2) **Iranian institutional counter-signal at maximum amplitude**: FM Baghaei said 'deep and significant disagreements remain' (Al Jazeera primary, IRNA); Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf publicly stated US is 'not an honest negotiating partner' (Al Jazeera primary); Iran UN mission in NY accused Washington of 'excessive demands' pushing talks toward collapse (Al Jazeera primary); Tasnim News Agency reported May 23, citing a military source, that 'Iran is preparing for resuming fighting with the United States and Israel' (Haaretz liveblog corroboration, Tasnim primary). **The Sadjadpour 'two hubris' frame is now at maximum institutional fanout** — civilian-government (Pezeshkian/Araghchi/Baghaei) procedural + legislative-conservative (Ghalibaf) rhetorical-maximalist + military-IRGC (Tasnim) kinetic-ready + supreme-leader (Mojtaba leak) nuclear-foreclosure = COORDINATED MULTI-TRACK POSTURE BY FUNCTION, NOT FACTIONAL SPLIT. (3) **Baghaei publicly framed deal timeline: '30 to 60 days' for finalizing MOU details** (Al Jazeera May 23 primary, Haaretz, IRNA) — first publicly-articulated Iranian deal timeline of cycle. Structurally LONGER than Trump's 'every day better' framing AND Al Arabiya leak's 'within 7 days' architecture. **The 30-60-day timeline is also long enough to push deal past Israeli unilateral-risk window (June 5) AND past second-IC-departure window (June 21) — Iran buying time inside deal-protected window.** Iran 'focused on finalizing memorandum of understanding with U.S.' but explicitly noted 'a conclusion would not be reached if the US sought to delve into details about highly enriched uranium in Iran' — structurally consistent with Mojtaba directive's substance without explicit attribution. (4) **Mojtaba uranium directive Day 2 of 14**: NO Iranian state-media outlet (IRNA, Tasnim, Mehr, Press TV) has confirmed, denied, or walked back the May 21 leaked directive through May 23. The Daily Sabah May 22 piece 'Iran supreme leader vows to keep enriched uranium in country' amplifies the original Reuters wire without adding independent sourcing. **The market has priced the directive as material**: gold fell to $4,510.60 Thursday on reports the directive was complicating the deal track. Trump's softer 'satisfactorily handled' formulation on uranium suggests US verbally accommodating directive's substance without formal deal. (5) **Dual-channel architecture operationally explicit**: Al Jazeera May 23 quoted an Iranian official describing Qatar's role as 'vital, important, and fundamental to supporting Pakistan's mediation' — Pakistan formal/military + Qatar substantive/IRGC backchannel is now-explicit architecture. (6) **IRGC May 23 claim: 25 vessels transited Hormuz** in past 24h with IRGC coordination/security (CLAIMED — IRGC primary, Al Jazeera secondary). Second consecutive daily claim in 25-35 range. Contradicted by Bloomberg/Lloyd's/UKMTO (continued 'largely frozen' framing); AWRP ~1% holding inconsistent with claim. The claim is propaganda lock-in for Iran-Oman 'permanent toll mechanism' architecture Rubio's May 21 red line rejected. (7) **CBS reports US military/intel personnel canceled Memorial Day weekend plans** (REPORTED, decision 'yet to be made'); symmetrically Tasnim military-source 'preparing for resumed fighting' — both sides administratively pre-positioning while procedural mediation moves through Munir-COAS channel. **Vali Nasr 'time on Iran's side' thesis operationally validated** by Trump's softer uranium framing — Iran did not have to publicly concede the nuclear track and US has verbally accommodated. **Falsifiable architecture**: deal deliverable inside 7 days (by 2026-05-30) = Trump's faster track wins, Iran was bluffing on 30-60-day; deliverable inside 30-60 days = Baghaei anchor operational; no deliverable by 2026-07-22 = Sadjadpour structural-mismatch confirmed.

recent_signals.headline: **MOJTABA KHAMENEI'S FIRST SUBSTANTIVE POLICY DIRECTIVE LEAKED MAY 21 — URANIUM ENRICHED TO 60% TO 'REMAIN STRICTLY INSIDE IRANIAN TERRITORY.' DIRECTLY FORECLOSES TRUMP'S MAY 16 NUCLEAR ULTIMATUM. PAKISTAN-MEDIATED DRAFT TEXT LEAKED VIA AL ARABIYA ADDRESSES HORMUZ/CEASEFIRE/SANCTIONS — BUT NOT URANIUM. IRGC THREATENED 'GLOBAL OFFENSIVE' MAY 21 (RHETORIC, NO ACTION). MOJTABA STATE-MEDIA SILENCE DAY 15; THE DIRECTIVE IS NOW THE VOICE.** (1) **Mojtaba directive (REPORTED — Reuters wire via Algemeiner/Kathmandu Post/CDM Press May 21)**: anonymous Iranian sources said Mojtaba ordered that Iran's stockpile of uranium enriched to 60% 'remain strictly inside Iranian territory' — 'the Supreme Leader's directive, and the consensus within the establishment, is that the stockpile of enriched uranium should not leave the country.' Reasoning per sources: Iran would be 'more vulnerable to future attacks by the United States and Israel' if material left. **Structurally the first publicly-attributed substantive policy directive from Mojtaba since his appointment in March, surfaced AT THE EXACT MOMENT the Pakistan-mediated framework is moving** — regime-internal signal to Trump that the nuclear track is foreclosed independent of operational deliverable architecture. (2) **Pakistan track operationally active**: Pakistan Interior Minister Naqvi met FM Araghchi in Tehran multiple times May 20-22 (CONFIRMED — Washington Times May 22, IRNA primary). Al Arabiya English May 22 leaked what it called the 'final draft' Pakistan-mediated text (REPORTED — Saudi state-influenced primary leak; Tasnim/ILNA confirmed draft moving but trace to same chain): immediate comprehensive unconditional ceasefire + Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz freedom-of-navigation guarantees + sanctions lifted in phases on Iranian compliance + talks within 7 days. **The text addresses Hormuz/ceasefire/sanctions but NOT uranium dismantlement** — Trump's May 16 letter central demand omitted. (3) **IRGC May 21 'global offensive' threat (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — Iran International May 21, The Week May 20, CBS News liveblog)**: 'war beyond Middle East' framing escalates beyond prior IRIB May 19 'retreat-based-on-fear' framing. **No operational follow-through verified May 21-22** (no missile launches, no proxy attack scaling beyond established patterns). Tag holds at RHETORIC. (4) **Iranian state-media institutional divergence operationally active**: Tasnim (IRGC-linked) ran Iran's counter-proposal procedural framing (per The Globe Security, NewKerala, ProKerala May 19-22) emphasizing the deliverables Iran wants — end of war / sanctions lift / frozen-assets release / end of US blockade / war reparations. IRNA (civilian-government-aligned) covered Naqvi-Araghchi meetings in operational-procedural framing without amplifying IRGC's 'global offensive' rhetoric. **The internal divergence flagged May 19 brief is now operationally active**: civilian-government procedurally engaged with Pakistan track; IRGC and Khamenei's office rhetorically + substantively NO. (5) **IRGC Hormuz transit claim May 22**: 35 vessels passed in 24 hours (CLAIMED — IRGC primary, Iran International secondary). Independent maritime trackers (hormuzstraitmonitor.com, IranWarLive, Bloomberg Hormuz Tracker May 17) continue to characterize commercial shipping as 'largely frozen' — 35 vessels claim likely refers to ALL transits including Iranian coastal/cabotage, NOT international VLCC/tanker traffic. 1,550 stranded vessels and 22,500 trapped mariners headline figure remains operative. (6) **Mojtaba public-silence trigger advances to Day 15** with the public-silence pattern intact but the leak-architecture-voice pattern now operative. The May 21 directive (if real) functions as REGIME POLICY without requiring Mojtaba to appear publicly. (7) **Rial divergence trigger (Day 5 of 5) resolving UNTRIPPED**: Alanchand 1,775K May 19, Bonbast last May 15 1,822K — divergence narrowing not widening; deal-anticipation capping decline pace; 2M trigger ETA pushed past mid-June. **Mechanism inside the deal architecture**: Trump's May 16 letter required full dismantlement + zero enrichment within 2 months; the leaked Al Arabiya text addresses Hormuz/ceasefire/sanctions but NOT uranium; Mojtaba's directive (if real) makes the substantive nuclear track non-deliverable. The asymmetric path Iran appears to want: ceasefire deliverable in days, nuclear deliverable foreclosed. **Sadjadpour 'two hubris' frame at maximum operational expression**: civilian (Pezeshkian/Araghchi) procedurally engaged via Pakistan; IRGC + Khamenei's office substantively NO. **Vali Nasr 'time on Iran's side' thesis operationally validated by Trump's 'no hurry' framing** — Iran did NOT have to publicly concede anything to keep the diplomatic track alive across the postponement window. The Mojtaba directive converts the nuclear-track non-deliverable from 'regime collapse risk' into a 'principled red line,' making it politically tenable to accept ceasefire-and-sanctions-lift while preserving the nuclear program. **Falsifiable**: Iran formally accepts the leaked Al Arabiya text within 7 days without nuclear amendments = thesis confirmed (Iran got the asymmetric split it wanted). Iran softens the Mojtaba directive within 14 days = thesis wrong (directive was bargaining noise, not a floor).

recent_signals.headline: **TRUMP POSTPONED MAY 19 STRIKE AT GULF REQUEST — IRAN STATE TV (IRIB) CALLED IT A 'RETREAT' BASED ON 'FEAR.' FM BAGHAEI CONFIRMED COUNTER-PROPOSAL DELIVERED THROUGH PAKISTAN; QESHM ISLAND DEFENSE SYSTEMS REPORTEDLY ACTIVATED LATE MONDAY.** Iran's two-track information posture operationally crystallized today: state media hardens the rhetorical frame ('retreat-based-on-fear' via IRIB broadcast ticker and X account); institutional channels keep procedurally moving the counter-proposal through Pakistan. **FM spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei (via Tasnim primary)**: Iran's response to recent US proposals was 'conveyed to the American side through mediator Pakistan' with enumerated demands: (i) unfreezing of frozen Iranian assets, (ii) lifting of US sanctions on Iranian oil sales, (iii) lifting of US blockade of Iranian ports, (iv) 'stoppage of fighting in all aspects' including cessation in Lebanon, and (v) compensation for war damages — far apart from US zero-enrichment red line. **The Pakistan channel is procedural-real, framework-draft-pending.** Trump's postponement post (late May 18 US time) credited Qatar Emir Tamim, Saudi MbS, and UAE MbZ for the request — Iran NOT publicly credited as initiator. **IRIB 'retreat based on fear' framing**: converts the Gulf-mediated postponement into an Iranian rhetorical win, deepening the trust deficit on the negotiating track. This is the cleanest publicly-stated example of the Iranian 'negotiate while reframing the postponement as adversary weakness' pattern — Sadjadpour's 'two hubris' frame applies in real-time. **Defense systems reportedly activated on Iran's Qeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz late Monday** (REPORTED single-source from Trump-postponement coverage), with Iranian officials reporting situation 'under control' — Qeshm is Iran's largest Gulf island, hosts IRGC Navy facilities and surface-to-air missile batteries; activation is a CONFIRMED operational-readiness posture against potential US/Israeli strikes through the Hormuz corridor. **Consistent with Trump's 'remain prepared for full-scale assault at a moment's notice'** — Iran reading the postponement as conditional and behaving accordingly. **A9 MOJTABA KHAMENEI DAY 12 STATE-MEDIA SILENCE**: CNN piece 'Iran's new supreme leader is nowhere to be seen — that might be helping the regime to survive' re-circulates April 21 framing. Pezeshkian's May 7 claim of meeting Mojtaba 'for two and a half hours' remains uncorroborated by any image, audio, or third-party witness. Tasnim Abdollahi-briefing May 17 was second state-media positioning act in five days naming Mojtaba in operational-command function without producing him. **Deepens the structural problem with the Pakistan mediation track**: even if a framework draft is produced, who in the Iranian institutional architecture has the authority to sign it? Mojtaba absence question now connects to the deal-execution question. **A12 RIAL Alanchand 1,775,000 IRR/USD May 19 (-0.11% from Monday 1,777K)** — past 24-hour range 1,775K-1,810K; past week 3.06% strengthening. Bonbast last May 15 print still 1,822K. The divergence is narrowing rather than widening — source-quality flag trigger resolves toward UNTRIPPED Day 5 of 5. The slight rial strengthening on the postponement news is operationally consistent: the Iranian black-market FX market reads Gulf-mediated postponement as marginally relieving (same direction as Brent). Hyperinflation-thesis (2M IRR/USD breakthrough) recedes further from threshold. **Vali Nasr 'time on Iran's side' thesis operationally validated**: Iran's strategic patience produced an external de-escalation force (Gulf leaders) that Iran did not have to publicly request.
